posts.text:
In Doha, Qatar to cover the U.S.-Israel war on #Iran that has devastated tens of millions of lives across this beautiful region.

Video

Told Chicago Sun Times that the attack on the elementary school in Minab is just another example of how little the West and its key Middle East ally care about protecting civilians when they bomb Muslim populations.
chicago.suntimes.com/columni…

Well explained @brhodes.

To understand, imagine the reverse: if #Iran bombed an elementary school in the US and killed 150+ children, the world would call it what it is - a horror and a crime.

Children in Minab deserve the same outrage. Moral clarity shouldn’t depend on whose children they are.


Ben Rhodes (@brhodes)

"I had never heard of Minab, Iran until my tax dollars paid for missiles that slammed into a school, roughly the size of my daughters’, killing well over a hundred children alon

Joined NPR @hereandnow to discuss the second week of US-Israeli war on Iran, the new supreme leader Ayatollah Khamenei, and how the Iranian society views it all. 

The full show: npr.org/2026/03/09/nx-s1-574…

Video

Joined @FRANCE24 to discuss media coverage of the US-Israel war on #Iran needs much improvement. Also journalists need to get access to report on the ground as @fpleitgenCNN just did in Iran.

Watch full show: piped.video/LqnFI-QnE1g?is=4qqZ…

Video
Great to be on set in Paris with @whilderbrandt. Merci 🙏🏽

Joined @CatoInstitute and discussed the second week of US-Israel war on Iran with @Hoffman8Jon, @gbrew24 and @citrinowicz.

WATCH: nitter.net/catoinstitute/status/2…




Cato Institute (@CatoInstitute)

Join @CatoInstitute for a conversation with leading experts on the possible trajectories of Operation Epic Fury and its broader consequences for the region and beyond. nitter.net/i/broadcasts/1NxarazaW…




— https://nitter.net/CatoInstitute/status/2031762971970412841#m

Joined Senator @BernieSanders for a long conversation about the US-Israel war on #Iran, the mounting civilian toll and destruction of infrastructure, and how ordinary Iranians are living under intensifying bombardment.
With @tparsi + @SinaToossi 

VIDEO: piped.video/-CHIR1J8Zq8?is=usOV…

Well put @vali_nasr. This is part of a survival strategy in Western societies with deep-rooted racism and Islamophobia, and the pressure to be the “good” immigrant by hyper-assimilating to the point of denying your own identity.


Raza Ahmad Rumi (@Razarumi)

Some segments of the Iranian diaspora cheering Iran’s destruction reflect a deeper identity politics. As Vali Nasr notes, they seek distance from the “Global South” and aspire to a Western—often white—political identity. Support for Israel
